vwap_rsi_ema.name: VWAP + RSI(3) + EMA(8) Intraday Stock Strategy
vwap_rsi_ema.description: Three indicators, each doing a different job. EMA(8) for trend direction, VWAP for session bias, RSI(3) for entry timing. All three must align before a trade fires. Designed for liquid US ETFs and large-caps on intraday timeframes.

indicators.ema_8: Trend direction. Price above EMA(8) = bullish momentum. Below = bearish.
indicators.vwap: Session bias. Price above VWAP = buyers in control for the session. Below = sellers. Anchored to one US trading session (~6.5h).
indicators.rsi_3: Entry timing. RSI on 3 candles only — extremely sensitive. Below 30 in an uptrend = snap-back setup. Above 70 in a downtrend = reversal likely.

bullish: Price is above VWAP
Price is above EMA(8)

long: Price is above VWAP — buyers in control for the session
Price is above EMA(8) — uptrend confirmed
RSI(3) has dropped below 30 — short-term pullback in an uptrend, snap-back likely
short: Price is below VWAP — sellers in control
Price is below EMA(8) — downtrend confirmed
RSI(3) has spiked above 70 — overbought in a downtrend, reversal likely

exit_rules: Exit when RSI(3) crosses back above 50 (for longs) or below 50 (for shorts)
Hard stop: 0.3% below entry for longs, 0.3% above entry for shorts
Take profit at next VWAP touch or EMA(8) cross
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade
Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
Do not trade if price is more than 1.5% away from VWAP — overextended
Only trade during US regular session (9:30am – 4:00pm ET)
vwap_rsi_ema.notes: Intraday strategy — designed for 5m to 15m timeframes on liquid US equities/ETFs (SPY, QQQ, AAPL, MSFT). RSI(3) is intentionally fast and sensitive. Do not apply to higher timeframes without re-tuning.

gap_and_go_cameron.name: Gap & Go (Ross Cameron / Warrior Trading) — Scanner
gap_and_go_cameron.description: Long-only momentum scanner — runs once per trading day on a curated universe of historically gap-prone names (small/mid-cap momentum, biotech, EV, crypto-adjacent, recent IPOs). For each symbol, the bot computes the overnight gap %, relative volume, and VWAP/EMA(9) hold; it ranks symbols by gap × relative-volume and surfaces the top 20 that pass the entry rules. Pre-market-high break and <20M-share float (Cameron's hand-traded filters) are noted but NOT gated — the bot can't see those from daily Questrade candles, so the scanner produces a candidate list rather than auto-trades.

indicators.premarket_gap_pct: Move from prior close to 9:29 ET price, expressed as a percent. Setup requires ≥4%.
indicators.relative_volume: Today's cumulative volume divided by the 50-day average at the same time of day. Setup requires ≥5×.
indicators.ema_9: Fast trend reference on 1m/5m. Pullbacks should hold above the 9 EMA.
indicators.vwap: Session anchor. Healthy gappers stay above VWAP after the open; a VWAP loss kills the trade.
indicators.float: Tradable shares. Cameron prefers <20M float for the cleanest momentum.

bullish: Pre-market gap ≥ 4% on a real catalyst
Relative volume ≥ 5× by 9:30 ET
Float < 20M shares preferred (works up to 100M with stronger catalyst)

long: First 1m or 5m candle after 9:30 ET breaks the pre-market high
Breakout candle prints volume ≥ 1× the 50-day average daily volume
Price is above VWAP and above the 9 EMA on the entry candle
Spread is < 0.2% of price (skip illiquid names)

exit_rules: Hard stop: low of the breakout candle (or 1% below entry, whichever is tighter)
Take profit at 2R (twice the dollar risk) — Cameron's standard winner size
Trail the 9 EMA on 1m chart once price is +1R; exit on a clean break and close below it
Cut at 11:00 ET regardless — momentum reliability falls off after the morning session
risk_rules: Risk ≤ 1% of account per trade; Cameron's PDT-account variant uses 0.5%
Maximum 3 trades per day — stop after two consecutive losers
Do not enter after 10:30 ET; the gap-and-go window is the first hour
Halt + resume bars: stand aside until the first post-halt 1m candle closes
Skip any stock with a pending earnings or FDA decision today
gap_and_go_cameron.notes: Per-winner target ≥5% of position notional is realistic when entry stop is ~1–2% and target is 2R. Backtesting on intraday Russell 2000 small-cap gappers is the most representative dataset. Requires real-time scanner feed (gappers list); will not fire correctly on a static watchlist of large-caps.

abcd_aziz.name: ABCD Pullback Continuation (Andrew Aziz)
sources: Andrew Aziz — Bear Bull Traders, 'How to Day Trade for a Living'
abcd_aziz.description: Trades the second leg of a momentum move. A→B is the initial vertical thrust, B→C is a measured pullback (typically 38–61% retrace), C→D is the continuation. Entry at C when the pullback shows reversal candles + volume drying up; exits at D = projected 1:2 R/R or first sign of failure.

indicators.swing_high_a_to_b: Identify the A→B move as a 5m candle range > 2× the 14-day ATR — i.e. an unusual thrust.
indicators.fib_retracement_b_to_c: Pullback should land between 38% and 61% of A→B before reversing.
indicators.vwap: The C-pivot must hold above VWAP for longs (below for shorts).
indicators.ema_20: 20-period on 5m chart — confluence with C is the highest-quality setup.
indicators.volume_drying_up: Pullback bars (B→C) should print sub-average volume; continuation candle prints surge volume.

bullish: A→B is a sharp upward thrust on heavy volume
B→C pullback is orderly (no consecutive heavy red bars)
C-pivot holds above prior intraday support / VWAP / 20 EMA
bearish: A→B is a sharp downward thrust on heavy volume
B→C bounce is weak (low volume, narrow bars)
C-pivot rejects below prior intraday resistance / VWAP / 20 EMA

long: Identify A (start) and B (top of thrust); thrust is >2× ATR(14)
Pullback retraces 38–61% of A→B and stalls at VWAP, 20 EMA, or prior breakout level
Reversal candle at C: bullish engulfing, hammer, or higher-low close
Enter on the next candle's break of the C-candle high
Volume on the entry candle exceeds the average of the prior 5 bars
short: A→B is a downward thrust >2× ATR(14)
B→C bounces 38–61% and stalls at VWAP, 20 EMA, or prior breakdown level
Reversal candle at C: bearish engulfing, shooting star, or lower-high close
Enter on the next candle's break of the C-candle low
Volume on entry exceeds the average of the prior 5 bars

exit_rules: Hard stop: just beyond C (a few cents past the pullback's extreme)
Target D: project the A→B distance from C — that's the 1:2+ measured move
Scale 50% at 1R; trail the rest with the 20 EMA on the entry timeframe
Time stop: exit if D not reached within 6× the A→B candle count
risk_rules: Risk ≤ 1% of account per trade
If the C-candle range is wider than 1% of price, position size shrinks to keep stop at 1% account risk
Skip if A→B move was overnight (pullback dynamics are different)
Skip if news is hitting the tape during the pullback — reverts to a different regime
Maximum 4 ABCDs per day — quality over quantity
abcd_aziz.notes: Aziz teaches this on the 1m or 5m. Per-winner expectancy of 5–8% on the position is achievable when the move is taken at 2R and ATR conditions hold. Avoid on FOMC / CPI release days — the regular ABCD geometry breaks down.

bull_flag_qullamaggie.name: Stage-2 Bull Flag Breakout (Kristjan Kullamägi)
bull_flag_qullamaggie.description: Hold-time 3–15 days. Looks for stocks that have just made a sharp vertical move (>30% in 1–3 months) followed by a tight consolidation near the highs (a 'bull flag'). Enters on day-1 breakout above the consolidation high with volume; rides the second leg of the move. Wins target 20–40% of position; losers are exited cleanly at the consolidation low.

indicators.ema_10_d: 10-day EMA — primary trail for the trend.
indicators.ema_20_d: 20-day EMA — secondary trail; deeper pullbacks are still valid above 20 EMA.
indicators.ma_50_d: 50-day SMA — the stock must be above this for stage-2 status.
indicators.ma_200_d: 200-day SMA — must be rising; price above it.
indicators.adr_pct: Average Daily Range over 20 days, in percent. Kristjan prefers ADR ≥ 5% for the asymmetry he wants.
indicators.relative_volume: Breakout day must show volume ≥ 1.5× the 50-day average.
indicators.prior_move: Must have a recent vertical move ≥ 30% within the last 1–3 months.

bullish: Stock has rallied 30%+ within 1–3 months prior to the consolidation
Price is above the rising 10/20 EMAs and the 50 SMA
200 SMA is rising and price is above it
Consolidation is 3–10 bars tight (high-low range < 50% of the prior move's leg)

long: Day-1 breakout: price closes above the consolidation high
Breakout day's volume ≥ 1.5× the 50-day average
Stock's ADR is ≥ 5% — provides headroom for the second leg
Optional intraday entry: 5m or 1H break of the consolidation high with volume

exit_rules: Hard stop: low of the consolidation (or low of the breakout day, whichever is tighter)
Sell 1/3 to 1/2 into strength after 3–5 days, when the move shows extension
Trail the rest with the 10 EMA on daily — exit on a daily close below it
Hard time-stop: exit any remainder after 15 trading days; second-leg moves rarely persist beyond that
risk_rules: Risk ≤ 0.25–0.5% of account per trade — Kristjan keeps risk small relative to many small-cap traders
Position size derived from stop distance, not a flat dollar amount
Skip if the broader market (SPY/QQQ) is in a clear stage-4 downtrend
Skip if breakout closes weakly (lower 1/3 of the day's range)
Avoid earnings within the next 5 trading days — gap risk vs. expected reward is asymmetric
bull_flag_qullamaggie.notes: Kristjan publishes his portfolio drawdowns and wins on his website. Median winner historically is 20–30% over 3–10 days when the setup fires cleanly, comfortably clearing the 5% target. The strategy LIVES on the small/mid-cap growth complex; running it on SPY/VOO will produce mostly false signals.

avwap_reclaim_shannon.name: Anchored VWAP Reclaim (Brian Shannon)
sources: Brian Shannon — AlphaTrends, 'Maximum Trading Gains with Anchored VWAP'
avwap_reclaim_shannon.description: Hold-time 1–10 days. Anchors a VWAP to a meaningful event (earnings gap, all-time high, swing low, IPO, merger). Trades the moment price RECLAIMS that AVWAP after spending time below it — buyers have absorbed all sellers from the anchor day, momentum flips. Stops are tight (just below the reclaim); targets are the next higher AVWAP, prior swing high, or a measured-move extension.

indicators.anchored_vwap_event: Anchor to: most recent earnings, all-time high, gap day, or material news day. The anchor IS the level that matters.
indicators.anchored_vwap_swing_low: Second AVWAP from the most recent significant swing low — confluence with the event AVWAP marks high-conviction zones.
indicators.volume: Reclaim must happen on volume — average or higher. Low-volume reclaims fail more often.
indicators.ema_20_h: 20-period EMA on the entry timeframe — secondary confirmation that the trend has flipped.
indicators.atr_14: For position sizing only — stop distance derived from ATR multiples below the AVWAP.

bullish: Price was below the event-AVWAP for ≥ 5 trading days
Price closes above the event-AVWAP on the entry timeframe
Reclaim candle volume ≥ 20-period average volume
20 EMA on entry timeframe begins to flatten or curl up
bearish: Price was above the event-AVWAP for ≥ 5 trading days
Price closes below the event-AVWAP on the entry timeframe with volume
20 EMA on entry timeframe begins to flatten or curl down

long: Identify the most relevant anchor (earnings, ATH, gap, swing low)
Wait for price to close above the AVWAP on the entry timeframe (1H or 4H)
Confirm volume on the reclaim bar is ≥ the 20-bar average
Enter at the close of the reclaim bar; alt entry on the first pullback that holds AVWAP
short: Mirror image: AVWAP loss with volume after sustained holding above it
Enter on close below AVWAP; alt entry on first lower-high that fails AVWAP

exit_rules: Hard stop: 1× ATR(14) below AVWAP for longs (above for shorts)
Target 1: prior swing high / next overhead AVWAP — book 1/2 here
Target 2: measured move = distance from anchor low to anchor — book remainder
Move stop to breakeven once price is +1R away
Exit fully if AVWAP is lost on a closing-bar basis after entry
risk_rules: Risk ≤ 1% of account per trade
Two AVWAPs (event + swing-low) at the same level = high-conviction; one alone = standard size
Skip if next earnings is within 3 trading days
Avoid if SPY is also losing its own AVWAP from a recent all-time high — broad-tape correlation kills idiosyncratic setups
avwap_reclaim_shannon.notes: Per-trade winners typically run 5–15% of position notional when the anchor is well-chosen and the move has room. Brian's central thesis: AVWAP reflects the average price PAID by everyone trading since the anchor — a reclaim flips them all from underwater to in profit, removing supply pressure. Manual anchor selection means this strategy resists naive automation; the bot would need a heuristic for picking the right anchor (latest earnings is the simplest).

vcp_breakout_minervini.name: VCP — Volatility Contraction Pattern Breakout (Mark Minervini)
sources: Mark Minervini — SEPA, 'Trade Like a Stock Market Wizard'
vcp_breakout_minervini.description: Hold-time 1–14 days for the breakout phase (longer holds become full position trades). Identifies stocks consolidating in 2–4 successive volatility contractions — each pullback shallower than the last, each on lighter volume. Enters on the pivot breakout out of the tightest contraction with a volume surge; cuts losses at 7–8% (Minervini's hard rule); rides winners into clear strength.

indicators.vcp_contractions: 2–4 successive pullbacks within a base, each smaller than the previous. T1: 25–35%, T2: 15–20%, T3: 8–12%, T4 (optional): 5%. Volume contracts in parallel.
indicators.ma_50_d: 50-day SMA — must be rising; price must be above it.
indicators.ma_150_d: 150-day SMA — must be above the 200-day; price above the 150.
indicators.ma_200_d: 200-day SMA — must be rising for ≥ 1 month.
indicators.relative_strength_rating: IBD-style RS rating ≥ 70 (top 30% of the market) at minimum, ≥ 80 preferred.

bullish: Stage 2 uptrend: price above rising 50/150/200 SMAs
Price within 25% of its 52-week high
Relative strength rating ≥ 70 (preferred ≥ 80)
Base shows 2–4 successive volatility contractions

long: Pivot point: the high of the final, tightest contraction
Buy the breakout above the pivot on intraday strength (don't wait for close on first try)
Breakout volume ≥ 1.4× the 50-day average daily volume
Tight pivot range: high-low of the final contraction < 5% of price
Optional confirmation: relative strength line breaks to a new high alongside the pivot break

exit_rules: Hard stop: 7–8% below entry (Minervini's universal rule), or below the pivot — whichever is tighter
Sell 1/3 to 1/2 into the first 15–25% strength move (typically days 5–10)
Trail the rest with the 20-day EMA on daily — exit on a closing break
Cut immediately if the breakout fails (price closes back inside the base)
risk_rules: Position size such that stop = 1.25–2% of account — Minervini's 'progressive exposure'
Pyramid only into trades already +5% in your favor
Avoid earnings within the next 10 trading days (gap-down risk dwarfs the upside)
Avoid breakouts in a market downtrend (use SPY 200 SMA as the regime filter)
Maximum 5 open positions at a time during the early phase of a bull cycle
vcp_breakout_minervini.notes: Minervini's documented track record averaged ~30% per winning swing during favorable cycles; per-trade winner targets of 15–25% over 1–3 weeks are typical. The 7–8% maximum loss rule is the keystone — without it the strategy degrades into 'buy and hope'. RS rating, base count, and contraction quality require fundamental + chart-pattern discipline that no out-of-the-box indicator captures perfectly; this is a discretionary strategy first, mechanical second.
